title: Lead AI Solutions Engineer
company_focus:
AI platform for developing, delivering, and governing
predictive and generative AI at scale
description:
Job Description:

DataRobot delivers AI that maximizes impact and minimizes
business risk. Our platform and applications integrate into
core business processes so teams can develop, deliver, and
govern AI at scale. DataRobot empowers practitioners to
deliver predictive and generative AI, and enables leaders to
secure their AI assets. Organizations worldwide rely on
DataRobot for AI that makes sense for their business — today
and in the future.

The Principal AI Solutions Engineer plays a pivotal role in
helping enterprise customers see, feel, and recognize the
value of agentic AI, fast. This role sits at the
intersection of business understanding, AI application
design, and polished product execution. The primary mission
is to rapidly design and build enterprise-grade MVP (Minimum
Viable Product) agentic AI applications, often within
days—that clearly demonstrate how AI agents can transform
real business processes.

You will partner closely with prospective customers, subject
matter experts, and pre-sales team members to turn ambiguous
business problems into compelling, production‑inspired
prototypes. These MVPs are not throwaway demos; they are
credible, extensible foundations that showcase technical
depth, strong UX/UI taste, and modern AI architecture, while
remaining pragmatic and fast to deliver.

Required Skills, Expertise & Experience

This role requires senior-level judgment: deep hands-on
building experience, strong customer-facing intuition, and
the ability to make pragmatic tradeoffs under real-world
constraints.

Core Technical Skills

- Strong hands-on experience building agentic AI applications
  using modern LLM frameworks and orchestration patterns.
- Solid backend engineering skills (e.g., Python, Agent
  Skills, MCP, OpenAI Agents SDK, Claude Code, Cursor).
- Experience designing and running Agent Skills, MCP servers,
  tool APIs, and external system integrations.
- Familiarity with agent tracing, observability, logging, and
  evaluation techniques for AI applications.

Frontend & Design Taste

- Proficiency with React and modern frontend tooling.
- Experience using shadcn/ui or similar design systems to
  create clean, enterprise-ready UIs.
- Strong UX instincts—able to simplify complex workflows and
  present AI behavior transparently and confidently.

Problem Solving & Communication

- Exceptional ability to move from ambiguity to clarity
  quickly.
- Strong verbal and written communication skills; comfortable
  leading technical conversations with non‑technical
  stakeholders.
- Ability to explain complex AI concepts in a grounded,
  value‑driven, and honest way.

Education & Experience

- 10+ years of experience in software engineering, applied AI,
  hands-on technical consulting, or technical product roles.
- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a
  related field—or equivalent practical experience.
- Advanced degree or research background in AI/ML is a plus,
  but not required.
- Ability to travel up to 25% in average to customer meetings,
  workshops, industry conferences, and events.

Compensation Statement

The U.S. annual on-target earnings (OTE) range for this
full-time position is between $275,000 and $335,000
USD/year. This range represents a combination of annual base
pay and targeted commission. Actual offers may be higher or
lower than this range based on various factors, including
(but not limited to) the candidate’s work location, job-
related skills, experience, and education.

The talent and dedication of our employees are at the core
of DataRobot’s journey to be an iconic company. We strive to
attract and retain the best talent by providing competitive
pay and benefits with our employees’ well-being at the core.
Here’s what your benefits package may include depending on
your location and local legal requirements: Medical, Dental
& Vision Insurance, Flexible Time Off Program, Paid
Holidays, Paid Parental Leave, Global Employee Assistance
Program (EAP) and more!

DataRobot Operating Principles:

- Wow Our Customers
- Set High Standards
- Be Better Than Yesterday
- Be Rigorous
- Assume Positive Intent
- Have the Tough Conversations
- Be Better Together
- Debate, Decide, Commit
- Deliver Results
- Overcommunicate

All DataRobot hires are required to complete a background
check prior to starting employment, which includes identity
verification, criminal history check, employment
verification and education verification. Additionally, all
DataRobot employees must be available to attend in-person
company trainings and meetings.

Research shows that many women only apply to jobs when they
meet 100% of the qualifications while many men apply to jobs
when they meet 60%. At DataRobot we encourage ALL
candidates, especially women, people of color, LGBTQ+
identifying people, differently abled, and other people from
marginalized groups to apply to our jobs, even if you do not
check every box. We’d love to have a conversation with you
and see if you might be a great fit.

DataRobot is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity and
Affirmative Action employer. We do not discriminate based
upon race, religion, color, national origin, gender
(including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical
conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender
expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an
individual with a disability, or other applicable legally
protected characteristics. DataRobot is committed to working
with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants
with physical and mental disabilities. Please see the United
States Department of Labor’s EEO poster and EEO poster
supplement for additional information.

All applicant data submitted is handled in accordance with
our Applicant Privacy Policy.
responsibilities: Design and build enterprise-grade MVP agentic AI applications rapidly, often within days, for prospective customers
Partner with customers, subject matter experts, and pre-sales teams to translate ambiguous business problems into compelling AI prototypes
Design and implement Agent Skills, MCP servers, tool APIs, and external system integrations
Create polished, production-inspired prototypes with strong UX/UI design that showcase modern AI architecture
Implement agent tracing, observability, logging, and evaluation for AI applications
Lead technical conversations with non-technical stakeholders to explain AI concepts in value-driven terms
Travel up to 25% to customer meetings, workshops, and industry conferences
use_cases: Enterprise agentic AI application prototyping for business process transformation
Customer-facing AI MVP development to demonstrate value of AI agents
Designing and orchestrating multi-agent workflows with tool integrations
Building credible, extensible AI application foundations for enterprise customers
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role requires hands-on building of agentic AI
applications using LLM frameworks, agent orchestration
patterns, MCP servers, and tool APIs. The engineer directly
designs and builds enterprise-grade MVP AI agent
applications, which constitutes working ON AI systems rather
than merely supporting them.
